message:
feat(kafka): replace offset counter with stub broker; implement real transactional staging

Kafka producer (src/messaging/kafka.rs):

  Replace the minimal next_stub_offset() counter with a proper
  StubBroker that maintains per-partition message logs in a
  BTreeMap<(topic, partition), Vec<StubBrokerRecord>>. The stub broker
  supports:

  - stub_broker_publish(): append a record and return real offset-based
    RecordMetadata; notifies waiters via Notify for consumer polling
  - stub_broker_fetch(): retrieve a record by topic/partition/offset
  - stub_broker_end_offset(): return the current high watermark
  - reset_stub_broker_for_tests(): clear all state between tests

  StubBrokerRecord carries topic, partition, key, payload, timestamp,
  and headers — matching the real Kafka record shape.

  Non-transactional send/send_with_headers now constructs a full
  StubBrokerRecord instead of discarding key/headers.

  Transactional producer overhauled:
  - TransactionalProducerState tracks active transaction flag,
    needs_abort sentinel, and a staged_records buffer (stub path)
  - begin_transaction() checks for stale transactions and sets active
  - send() stages records into the buffer instead of immediately
    publishing
  - commit_transaction() atomically publishes all staged records to the
    stub broker, then clears the staging buffer
  - abort_transaction() discards staged records without publishing
  - Custom Debug impl shows active/needs_abort state

  run_kafka_blocking() helper dispatches to the blocking pool when
  available, falling back to spawn_blocking_on_thread(). The kafka
  feature path uses run_kafka_transaction_op() for init/begin/commit/
  abort operations.

Kafka consumer (src/messaging/kafka_consumer.rs):

  Major expansion of the stub consumer to drive against the new stub
  broker instead of returning immediate errors:

  - StubConsumerState tracks per-partition committed offsets (BTreeMap),
    assigned partitions, and subscription topics
  - poll() fetches the next unread record from the stub broker by
    scanning assigned partitions in order; returns Poll::Pending and
    registers a waker when no records are available
  - commit() advances committed offsets for all assigned partitions to
    their current read positions
  - assign_partitions()/revoke_partitions() manage the assignment set
  - subscribe() records topic subscriptions and auto-assigns partition 0
  - Consumer group management stubs (join/leave/rebalance) updated with
    proper state tracking

  Tests added covering: poll-after-produce, commit-advances-offset,
  partition assignment/revocation, subscribe auto-assignment, group
  join/leave lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
